_contract: E2's judging rule, Harald's GATE-2 decision (option A, 2026-07-21). A calibration cue PASSES when BOTH hold: (1) the winning entry is in that cue's accept_set, and (2) the designated entry is inside the nomination window (top-K, K=12). Judged on the UNION (keyword/identity + embeddings), which is what Stage 4 ships - cue 11 exists precisely because embeddings alone fail it while the symbol path answers it exactly.
_frozen: 2026-07-21, at C0, BEFORE any Stage-4 retrieval code exists. Freezing before the code is what stops the gate becoming self-marking. Changing a set later requires the change and its reason to be recorded in dossier-27 and re-audited.
_method: For each cue the top-8 semantic candidates were read on their CONTENT and judged individually; uncertain candidates were opened in full before a verdict. Sets are deliberately CONSERVATIVE: a wrongly-omitted entry makes the gate stricter (a false failure), a wrongly-included one makes it laxer - so doubt resolves to exclusion.

_baseline_at_C0.winner_in_accept_set: 10 of 12 (union; 9 of 12 on embeddings alone - cue 11 is rescued by the symbol path)

_corrections.2026-07-21-keyword_cue_field: Added the keyword_cue_field carried by gen_stage0.py's CALIB but omitted in transcription. cue-11 is queried by SYMBOL (as C0 measured it); all others by symptom. NO accept_set was changed - this corrects the cue's query form, not what counts as a right answer.
